baseline_target_math.note: Worked once here so every scenario below can reference it. Implements D6.
baseline_target_math.double_count_check: reserved_slots (3) is NOT deducted alongside confirmed_inbound (2). Counting both would understate projected inventory by 2 and shrink the requirement from 4 to 2 -- the exact defect D6 prevents.

scenarios.name: Realistic target achieved by balanced plan

assertions: target_ending_inventory = floor(14 x 0.85) = 11; requirement is small
P(target achieved) >= 0.70 under BALANCED
all three plans are returned even though BALANCED suffices -- the margin-versus-capacity choice belongs to the general manager

scenarios.name: Target not achievable within event window

assertions: target_ending_inventory = 7, requiring 6 incremental sales in a 5-day window
feasibility.alternatives is non-empty and every entry carries a QUANTIFIED change with a unit
returning NOT_ACHIEVABLE without quantified alternatives would leave the merchandising manager exactly where they started
each alternative reports resulting_probability_target_achieved
only 3 eligible candidates remain, below min_safe_candidates (5)
the skill stops before building plans that cannot work
excluded_vehicles still reports every exclusion with its reason
historical_demand_lift 1.42 from lift_basis 3 prior events is applied
lift_confidence is MEDIUM, not HIGH -- three events is thin evidence
the lift multiplier applies only within the event window
historical_demand_lift is null, so the configured default 1.35 is used
lift_confidence is LOW
how much a past event's lift generalizes to a different event type is unaddressed by this model (open-questions.md C3), and the warning is the honest surfacing of that
partner_funded_incentives of $4,000 are EXCLUDED from the dealer budget check
they are INCLUDED in the customer-facing promotion_price
totals.total_dealer_funded is compared against budget_limit; total_partner_funded is not
a plan can be within budget while offering discounts exceeding the dealer-funded budget
CAPACITY_FIRST requires more dealer-funded discount than the $3,000 budget
the plan is still RETURNED, truncated at budget, rather than suppressed
totals.within_budget is false
PROMOTION_BUDGET_EXCEEDED is MEDIUM, not BLOCKING -- exceeding budget is a decision for the general manager, not a publication bar
V-10001 and V-10007 form a duplicate group: same model, year within 1, same trim, mileage within 15,000 (42,000 vs 39,000)
a plan discounting both triggers the warning
because plans are simulated JOINTLY, the cross-elasticity suppression appears in incremental_units_sold automatically rather than needing a separate adjustment
incremental units from discounting both is strictly less than the sum of discounting each alone
the requirement forces discounts into the emergency markdown reserve (last 20% of headroom)
approvals_required includes EMERGENCY_MARKDOWN
user confirmation does NOT substitute for manager approval -- the two gates are independent
V-10012 (130 days) is the expected first candidate to reach this reserve
even CAPACITY_FIRST leaves projected_ending_inventory.p50 above target_ending_inventory (8)
P(target achieved) falls between 0.30 and 0.70
gross_impact is negative and is stated as a cost, not framed as a tradeoff

scenarios.name: Vehicle expected to sell before the event is excluded

assertions: the Labor Day event starts 45 days after as_of, so vehicles with P50 days to sale under 45 are excluded
V-10001 and V-10007 are expected to be excluded on this rule
discounting a vehicle that would have sold at full price is a pure gross giveaway -- the most common way an event destroys margin
the threshold is this design's choice, not a specification requirement

scenarios.name: Baseline uses the same seed as the plans

assertions: a plan selecting ZERO vehicles yields incremental_units_sold of exactly 0 in every draw
without a shared seed, an empty plan would show nonzero incremental units from sampling noise alone
all three plans and the baseline report the same seed in their simulationRef
this is the regression test for seed sharing
